get.description:
Lightweight liveness check for container orchestrators (Kubernetes, Docker, ECS).

      **Scope**
      Verifies only that the process is alive and serving HTTP traffic.
      Does **not** check DB or Redis connectivity — use `/readiness` for that.

      **Performance**
      Constant-time, no I/O. Safe to poll every 1–5 seconds.

200.description: Process is alive and accepting requests.

503.description: Process is starting up, shutting down, or unhealthy.

get.description:
Returns the current top-10 bid and ask levels for the given trading pair.

      **Ordering — standard exchange layout**
      Both `bids` and `offers` are ordered **highest price first**.

      - `bids[0]` is the **best bid** (highest buy, closest to spread).
      - `offers[offers.length - 1]` is the **best ask** (lowest sell, closest to spread).

      Renders directly into two stacked tables: iterate each array top-to-bottom,
      put the asks (`offers`) above the bids, and the spread row sits between
      `offers[last]` and `bids[0]`.

      **Totals**
      `totals.bidTotalVolume` / `askTotalVolume` are cumulative notional values
      (sum of `price × amount` across the visible side) — use them as the
      denominator for depth-bar widths.
Returns every order for the authenticated client with status `OPEN` or `PENDING`.

Returns orders that match the provided filters. Results are always
      scoped to the authenticated client.

      Standard list query params are also supported: `fields`, `s`,
      `filter`, `or`, `sort`, `join`, `limit`, `offset`, `page`,
      `cache`.

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: Filter by status. Accepts repeated keys or comma-separated. Values are uppercased on transform.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by order type. Currently only `LIMIT` is supported.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (inclusive upper bound, must be ≥ `start_date`).

400.description: Validation error (invalid enum, malformed date-time, `start_date > end_date`, …).

get.description: Returns every order for the authenticated client, unfiltered by status. Intended for client-side P&L computation.
get.summary: Get the caller’s full order history

get.description: Returns the order with the given id, scoped to the authenticated client. Returns `null` when no such order exists.

404.description: No order with that id belongs to the caller.

patch.description: Cancels the order and marks it `CANCELLED`. Subsequent calls are no-ops.

200.description: On success the envelope `data` is the id of the cancelled order.

404.description: No order with that id belongs to the caller.

get.description: Returns a deposit address for the supplied coin, bound to the given platform user. Repeated calls for the same `(symbol, user)` pair may return the same address.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Platform user id this address is bound to.

get.summary: Generate a deposit address for a coin

get.description: Returns every deposit owned by the authenticated client.
Returns every coin that appears in an available product, paired with the list of trading products it participates in.

Returns historical OHLCV candles for the given product, ordered oldest-first.

      **`timeframe` is the candle bucket size**, not a history length.
      The server picks an appropriate Coinbase Advanced Trade granularity
      and a sensible lookback per timeframe so the chart shows ~100–300 candles:

      | `timeframe` | Granularity (Advanced Trade) | Candles returned | Total span |
      | --- | --- | --- | --- |
      | `1m`  | ONE_MINUTE      | 240 | 4 hours    |
      | `5m`  | FIVE_MINUTE     | 288 | 24 hours   |
      | `15m` | FIFTEEN_MINUTE  | 192 | 2 days     |
      | `1h`  | ONE_HOUR        | 168 | 1 week     |
      | `4h`  | TWO_HOUR        | 144 | 12 days    |
      | `1d`  | ONE_DAY         | 90  | 3 months   |
      | `1w`  | ONE_DAY         | 180 | ~6 months  |
      | `1M`  | ONE_DAY         | 270 | ~9 months  |
      | `1Y`  | ONE_DAY         | 290 | ~10 months |

      Coinbase Advanced Trade caps a single response at ~300 candles and
      has no native `FOUR_HOUR` / weekly / monthly / yearly granularity.
      `4h` is approximated with `TWO_HOUR` candles, and the longer
      labels use daily candles with progressively wider lookbacks.

      The legacy `granularity` query param is accepted but ignored — the
      server derives it from `timeframe` to prevent the two from drifting.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: Candle bucket size. The server picks the Coinbase granularity and a sensible lookback for each value — see the operation description for the table.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: Ignored — kept for backward compatibility. The server derives the effective granularity from `timeframe`. Older clients can still send a CandlesGranularity value; new code should just omit this.

get.summary: Get OHLCV candles for a product

get.description: Same products as `GET /product`, without the base/quote grouping.

post.description: Internal: re-fetches Coinbase assets and products and upserts them into the shared catalogue (preserving curation flags). Returns the counts touched.
post.summary: Refresh the local asset/product catalogue from Coinbase

get.description: Same shape as `GET /product`, filtered to a single coin. Returns `null` when the symbol is unknown.

404.description: No product known for the given symbol.

get.summary: Get product grouping by coin symbol

get.description:
Returns the assets you can trade — the base and quote assets of the
      products enabled for your account. With no product whitelist this is every
      asset used by the platform's enabled products; with a whitelist it narrows
      to the assets your allowed products use.

      **Caching**
      Response is suitable for client-side caching; the asset catalog changes infrequently.

Returns the markup/fee applied to the authenticated client’s fills, as a percent (e.g. 0.30 = 0.30%). This is the rate actually charged — a per-client value when configured, otherwise the platform default.
Returns the authenticated client’s effective fee plus the products it may trade with their order-value limits. Orders that violate these are rejected with a 400.

xenios-hmac.name: X-API-KEY
xenios-hmac.description: HMAC-SHA256 authentication. Sign every request and send X-API-KEY, X-API-SIGNATURE, X-API-TIMESTAMP and X-API-NONCE (see the Authentication guide). Each route also requires a specific claim on your API key (e.g. read:orders, write:orders, read:account, read:market-data).

statusCode.description: HTTP status code echoed in the body.

price.description: Limit price for this level (in quote currency).

amount.description: Aggregated quantity available at this price.

bidTotalVolume.description: Sum of (price × amount) across every visible bid level — i.e. the cumulative notional resting on the bid side.

askTotalVolume.description: Sum of (price × amount) across every visible ask level — cumulative notional resting on the ask side.

bids.description: Bid side — **highest price first**. `bids[0]` is the best bid (closest to the spread). Iterate top-to-bottom to render the bid table below the spread row.

offers.description: Ask side — **highest price first** (so the array iterates top-to-bottom in the standard exchange layout). The **best ask** sits at `offers[offers.length - 1]`, immediately above the spread row.

totals.description: Cumulative notional resting on each side of the book.

spread.description: Absolute spread between best bid and best ask (quote ccy).

spreadPercent.description: Spread as a percentage of the best ask.

ticker.description: Latest known ticker snapshot for this pair.

externalId.description: Exchange-side reference id assigned once the order is accepted.

userId.description: Id of the client that owns this order.

portfolioId.description: Portfolio id the order was placed under.

clientOrderId.description: Client-generated idempotency key (UUID) supplied on create.

type.description: Order type. Currently only `LIMIT` is supported.

filledValue.description: Filled value in quote asset (string-encoded decimal).

netAverageFilledPrice.description: Average filled price net of fees (string-encoded).

synced.description: True once the latest order state has been reconciled with the exchange.

totalUserRecieveAmount.description: Final amount credited to the client. For `BUY` this is base asset; for `SELL` it is quote asset (string-encoded).

portfolioId.description: Portfolio id the deposit was credited to.

client_id.description: Id of the client that owns this deposit.

apiEnabled.description: Whether this product is exposed through the public API.

coinbaseProductId.description: The exact Coinbase product id this pair resolves to (e.g. the stablecoin-quoted equivalent). Used when placing orders on Coinbase.

coinbaseMinSize.description: Coinbase minimum order size in base units.

coinbaseMaxSize.description: Coinbase maximum order size in base units.

coinbaseQuoteMinSize.description: Coinbase minimum order size in quote (notional) units — the real practical floor Coinbase enforces, e.g. 1 USD.

coinbaseQuoteMaxSize.description: Coinbase maximum order size in quote (notional) units.

minOrderSize.description: Platform minimum order value in quote (notional) units. Must be >= the Coinbase quote minimum.

maxOrderSize.description: Platform maximum order value in quote (notional) units. Must be <= the Coinbase quote maximum.

name.description: Base or quote asset symbol (e.g. BTC, USD).

availablePairs.description: All pairs (Products) where this asset appears.

start.description: Candle start (ISO 8601 or unix seconds, see source).

open.description: Opening price of the period (quote currency).

high.description: Highest traded price during the period.

low.description: Lowest traded price during the period.

volume.description: Total traded volume during the period (base currency).

tradingSupported.description: Whether this asset can be traded.

explorerUrl.description: URL pattern for an on-chain block explorer.

xenios_available.description: Whether the Xenios platform currently lists this asset.

markupPercent.description: Effective trading fee (markup) applied to the caller’s fills, as a percent. Example: 0.30 means 0.30%.

quoteCurrency.description: Currency the order-value limits are expressed in.

minOrderSize.description: Minimum order value in the quote currency. Null = no minimum (Coinbase floor applies).

maxOrderSize.description: Maximum order value in the quote currency. Null = no maximum (Coinbase ceiling applies).

markupPercent.description: Effective trading fee (markup) as a percent.

restricted.description: True when a product whitelist is in effect (the products below are the only ones tradable). False = unrestricted (the products below are all enabled products).

products.description: The products the caller may trade, each with its effective order-value limits.
